Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significantly elevated creatinine and microalbumin/creatinine ratio, indicating impaired renal function and possible nephropathy. Urinalysis reveals marked proteinuria, bilirubinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting renal and possible hepatic involvement. Low HbA1c is unusual given glucosuria; consider analytical or clinical context. Mild metabolic acidosis is present. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying causes, including diabetes, liver dysfunction, and possible urinary tract pathology.
